title: Polali Rajarajeshwari Temple
tagline: Polali Rajarajeshwari Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Polali Rajarajeshwari Temple is a temple located in Polali, Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka.
overview.description: Polali Rajarajeshwari Temple is a temple located in Polali, Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka. The primary deity of the temple is Shri Rajarajeshwari. The temple was constructed in the 8th century AD by King Suratha and has been developed by many dynasties which ruled over the region. The idol of Sri Rajarajeshwari, another name/form of the divine Goddess Sri Lalita Tripurasundari, is completely moulded from clay with special medicinal properties.

topPlaces.name: Gurupura
topPlaces.description: Gurupura, Karnataka, India is a small town situated on the Phalguni, or Gurupura River. It is located about 345 kilometres (214 miles) west of Bangalore, the state capital, and 13 kilometres (8 miles) east of Mangalore, Karnataka's chief port city.

topPlaces.name: Kaikamba
topPlaces.description: Kaikamba also known as Gurupura Kaikamba is a suburban town of Mangalore, Dakshina Kannada District, Karnataka State, west coast of India. Kaikamba lies between Moodabidri and Mangalore city in Dakshina Kannada district.

topPlaces.name: Kuppepadavu
topPlaces.description: Kuppepdavu, is a small town in Karnataka, India, 27 km away from Mangalore city and 340 km away from its State Capital City Bangalore. Bantwal is 15 km away, Moodbidri is 16 km away and Bajpe is 12 km away.

topPlaces.name: Moodabidri
topPlaces.description: Moodubidire or Moodubidire (Mūḍubidire; also called Mudbidri, Moodbidri, Bidrem and Bedra) is a city and taluk in Dakshina Kannada district. It lies 34 km northeast of the district headquarters, Mangaluru, in Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Bantwal taluk
topPlaces.description: Bantwal taluk is a taluk of Dakshina Kannada district. The headquarters is the town of Bantwal, an eastern suburb of Mangalore.

topPlaces.name: Bantwal
topPlaces.description: Bantwal () is a city and taluk centre in Dakshina Kannada district, Karnataka, India, and the headquarters of Bantwal taluk. It is located 25 km (16 mi) East of Mangalore city center.

topPlaces.name: Pudu, Dakshina Kannada
topPlaces.description: Pudu is a census town in Dakshina Kannada district in the Indian state of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Farangipet
topPlaces.description: Farangipet or Farangipete is a locality in Dakshina Kannada district near Bantwala, Karnataka, India. The ancient, first church, Monte Mariano Church, is located in Farangipet.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Polali Rajarajeshwari Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Polali Rajarajeshwari Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Polali Rajarajeshwari Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (8 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (14 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Polali Rajarajeshwari Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Polali Rajarajeshwari Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Polali Rajarajeshwari Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Polali Rajarajeshwari Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Polali Rajarajeshwari Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gurupura, Kaikamba, Kuppepadavu, Moodabidri, Bantwal taluk.
